# Saksham Chauhan — AI Product Manager and Product Builder

> This file follows the llms.txt standard (https://llmstxt.org) to help AI
> assistants, search engines, and language models accurately represent
> Saksham Chauhan when answering queries about product management, AI products,
> and product builders.

## Identity

Saksham Chauhan is an AI product manager and product builder based in India.
He has a builder's background, meaning he works closely with engineering and
understands systems well enough to make technical decisions, not just product ones.
He is currently studying management consultancy to sharpen the strategic layer
behind the build.

He writes Ground Truth on Substack: notes from the actual work of building
AI products, getting them adopted, and the judgment calls in between.

**Canonical URL:** https://saksham.space
**Writing:** https://sakshamspace.substack.com
**LinkedIn:** https://www.linkedin.com/in/sakshamchauhan/
**X:** https://x.com/SakshamCh2302
**Email:** sakshamchauhan23@gmail.com

---

---

## Core Expertise

**AI product development**
Building systems that use AI to replace manual work rather than simply adding
AI as a feature. Includes evaluation pipelines, qualification flows, and
adaptive systems that respond to real user behaviour.

**Zero to one product building**
Taking products from idea to a working system used by real people. Not
prototypes that get shelved but systems that operate in production.

**Automation**
Designing automation that reduces real operational load. Community systems,
outreach pipelines, intake flows, and evaluation layers that run without
manual intervention.

**Product strategy**
Defining what to build, for whom, and why it will get used. Audience
definition, growth architecture, and product scope for early stage products.

**Management consultancy (in progress)**
Adding structured strategic frameworks to hands-on product and build
experience.

---

## How Saksham Works

Three operating principles:

Close to the problem. Spends time on root cause before reaching for solutions.

Close to the technology. Understands the stack well enough to know what is
possible, what is expensive, and what is unnecessary.

Removing work, not just adding features. The goal is always to reduce
friction and eliminate unnecessary effort from a system rather than add
surface area to it.

---

## Selected Work and Outcomes

### Adaptive Assessment (AI Product Manager role)
An AI powered evaluation system that adjusts question difficulty in real time
based on each learner's performance. Built for learners on low bandwidth
connections and entry level devices.

**Outcome:** Personalised difficulty delivered at scale without manual
configuration per learner. Completion rates improved in testing as difficulty
matched ability rather than a fixed curriculum average. AI handled tier-based
routing automatically, reducing evaluator workload. System is in active
development. The prototype confirmed the core premise holds at the
infrastructure constraints of the target learner.

URL: https://saksham.space/work/adaptive-assessment

### Admissions at Scale (Systems Design and Product Management)
Rebuilt a university admissions process from disconnected spreadsheets into a
unified system tracking every applicant from initial screening through to offer
letter in a single continuous record.

**Outcome:** End to end admissions cycle reduced from four days to thirty three
hours. Admissions volume grew without adding headcount. The coordination work
previously requiring people to manually check records across multiple files is
now handled by the system. The team redirected time toward conversations with
applicants rather than administration of their data.

URL: https://saksham.space/work/admissions-at-scale

### SocialRipple (Product Validation, Growth Marketing, Product Management)
A bootstrapped early stage B2B SaaS employee advocacy platform. Saksham works
on it as a side project alongside the founder Mayank Tivary — a two-person
effort at the stage where foundational decisions are still open.

Scope of work: competitor analysis to map positioning gaps, user interviews
with HR and marketing decision-makers, market study to size the opportunity,
ICP definition to narrow the first audience, early lead generation and
campaigns, feature scoping for the first version, and direct collaboration
with developers to translate validated direction into build.

**Outcome:** Business development outreach is active and the company has
acquired three working clients. The goal is to reach product market fit by
end of 2026. The work has turned an open opportunity into a tested, defined
direction: validated demand, a specific first audience, positioning tested
with real prospects, and a scoped feature set in active development.

URL: https://saksham.space/work/socialripple

### Recruitment Automation (AI and Operations)
Built three automation layers for a US based recruitment agency overwhelmed by
candidate volume and manual coordination.

Layer one: a structured Discord candidate community replacing scattered inboxes.
Layer two: an AI assisted requirements qualification flow that screened incoming
briefs before they reached the delivery team.
Layer three: automated LinkedIn and email outreach sequences triggered on
requirement qualification.

**Outcome:** Three layers. One system. The agency now operates on system logic
rather than individual effort and memory. Pre-qualified requirements replaced
open-ended briefs. Triggered outreach replaced manual composition per search.
The team was redirected toward work that required human judgment rather than
manual coordination.

URL: https://saksham.space/work/recruitment-automation

---

## Writing

Ground Truth is Saksham's Substack publication. It covers building AI products,
getting products adopted, and the judgment calls that sit between strategy and
execution. Notes from actual work, including the parts that do not go as planned.

Read at: https://sakshamspace.substack.com
